annotations.rubric_point: Sub-points/arguments in the outline are merely descriptive and lack analytical depth
annotations.anchor_quote: 1. Only educated female breaks free from the shackles of patriarchal mind set that demands blind obedience.
annotations.correction: 1. Only an educated female breaks free from the shackles of the patriarchal mindset that demands blind obedience by critically analyzing and challenging imposed norms.
annotations.comment: Sub-point is merely descriptive and lacks analytical depth; add explanation of how education enables breaking free from patriarchal mindset.

annotations.rubric_point: Sub-points/arguments in the outline are merely descriptive and lack analytical depth
annotations.anchor_quote: 2. Educated female secures economic independence which protects from obeying mindlessly
annotations.correction: 2. An educated female secures economic independence, which protects her from obeying mindlessly by enabling self-reliant decision-making.
annotations.comment: Sub-point is merely descriptive and lacks analytical depth; include how economic independence leads to independent choices.

annotations.anchor_quote: there will be end to blind obedience
annotations.correction: there will be an end to blind obedience
annotations.comment: Missing article 'an' before 'end'; insert 'an' for grammatical correctness.

annotations.anchor_quote: stream line women education
annotations.correction: streamline women's education
annotations.comment: Incorrect spacing in 'stream line' and missing possessive 's'; change to 'streamline women's education'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Females strengthened by education lencew their rights
annotations.correction: Females strengthened by education know their rights
annotations.comment: Spelling error 'lencew' should be 'know'; correct to 'know their rights'.

annotations.anchor_quote: It is an undeniable reality that by enlarging the female mind with education, there will be end to blind obedience, yet there are Various reasons which inhibit the process; consequently women across the globe suffer detrimental impacts.
annotations.correction: It is an undeniable reality that by enlarging the female mind with education, there will be an end to blind obedience, yet there are various reasons which inhibit the process; consequently, women across the globe suffer detrimental impacts.
annotations.comment: Introduction addresses key terms but has grammar issues and unnecessary capitalization; refine for clarity and correct 'Various' to 'various' and add comma after 'consequently'.

annotations.anchor_quote: 5. Massive digital in present age of online learning. Case in Point: Global digital divide 68%, for women 51% (3) Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48% 6. Formales lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.correction: Develop sub-points with analytical explanations of how impediments cause blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Sub-points list descriptive case studies without analytical depth; add cause-effect analysis linking impediments to female education barriers.

annotations.anchor_quote: Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas mode's is 48%
annotations.correction: Pakistan female digital divide 24% whereas males' is 48% (cite source).
annotations.comment: Factual statistic lacks source attribution; cite reliable source like UN or World Bank.

annotations.anchor_quote: Formales lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.correction: Females' lack of security outside the four walls of their home.
annotations.comment: 'Formales' misspelled and lacks possessive; correct to 'Females''.

annotations.anchor_quote: Ensure "altidunal" change rather than more LawsD TAIMOOR BUKHARI
annotations.correction: Ensure 'attitudinal' change rather than more laws.
annotations.comment: 'Altidunal' misspelled and 'LawsD' erroneous; correct to 'attitudinal' and 'laws'.

annotations.anchor_quote: mitigate digital divide so that women from for flung areas can educate themselves.
annotations.correction: Mitigate digital divide so that women from far-flung areas can educate themselves.
annotations.comment: 'Mitigate' uncapitalized and 'for flung' misspelled; correct to 'Mitigate' and 'far-flung'.

annotations.anchor_quote: 1. Societal barriers and poverty Case in point: (1) Prioritizing boy's education over girl's (2) Not having the means to support education.
annotations.comment: Sub-points list descriptive case examples without analytical depth; add analysis linking them to perpetuating blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: Case in point: (1) Prioritizing boy's education over girl's (2) Not having the means to support education.
annotations.comment: Supporting details are narrative listings lacking critical analysis; explain cause-effect relationship to female subjugation.

annotations.anchor_quote: (2) In Pakistan 18% girls in age 12-16 get married.
annotations.comment: Factual statistic lacks source attribution; cite reliable source such as UN Women or Pakistan Bureau of Statistics.

annotations.anchor_quote: Females economic dependence on others to provide for their education.
annotations.correction: Females' economic dependence on others to provide for their education.
annotations.comment: Missing possessive form; use 'Females'' to correctly indicate possession.

annotations.anchor_quote: Case in point: Patriarch head of the family SYED TAIN decides and provides.
annotations.comment: Personal name reference is irrelevant to general argument; replace with generic patriarchal example.

annotations.anchor_quote: It has, undeniably, wisely phrased that it needs education to break free females from blind obedience.
annotations.correction: It has been undeniably and wisely phrased that education is needed to free females from blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Introduction restates topic but lacks concise overview of main arguments and roadmap; add explicit summary of benefits, impediments, and measures to guide reader.

annotations.anchor_quote: questions, thinks, rationalizes and only then make an action.
annotations.correction: questions, thinks, rationalizes, and only then makes an action.
annotations.comment: Subject-verb agreement error with singular 'one'; change 'make' to 'makes' for consistency.

annotations.anchor_quote: By enlarging the femal mind is by virtue of educating them makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.correction: Enlarging the female mind through education makes them better not only for their own good but also for the entire society.
annotations.comment: Awkward and grammatically incorrect structure; rephrase for clarity and correct spelling of 'female'.

annotations.anchor_quote: They question and rationalize and only than decide on whether to comply or not.
annotations.correction: They question authority before deciding whether to comply or not.
annotations.comment: Repeats earlier description of educated questioning without new depth; consolidate to avoid redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: They suffer socially, politically and economically and generational cycles of women
annotations.correction: They suffer socially, politically, and economically, perpetuating generational cycles of deprivation.
annotations.comment: Paragraph ends abruptly mid-thought; complete sentence and add transition to next section.

annotations.anchor_quote: deprivation neves break.
annotations.correction: deprivation never breaks.
annotations.comment: Fix spelling error 'neves' to 'never' to ensure readability.

annotations.anchor_quote: However, hot all hope is lost
annotations.correction: However, not all hope is lost
annotations.comment: Correct misspelling 'hot' to 'not' for accurate expression.

annotations.anchor_quote: what women cando, or can not;
annotations.correction: what women can do, or cannot;
annotations.comment: Insert space in 'cando' to 'can do' and fix parallel structure for clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: They do not let women think once for her own self and
annotations.correction: They do not let women think once for themselves, enabling independent decision-making.
annotations.comment: Complete abrupt paragraph ending with full thought or transition to next point.

annotations.anchor_quote: This mind set decides what women cando, or can not; what women can wear or wear not; what women can feel or feel not.
annotations.correction: Analyze how education empowers women to challenge these patriarchal controls through critical thinking and examples.
annotations.comment: Transform descriptive list into critical analysis with cause-effect links to strengthen argument.

annotations.anchor_quote: Conclusion.
annotations.correction: Expand with summary of main arguments, restatement of thesis, and forward-looking statement.
annotations.comment: Conclusion section in outline lacks detail compared to body sections; add structured points to balance and preview essay closure.

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. In Pakistan, ensure implementation of Article 25, and Article 38 NV.
annotations.correction: Analyze how implementation addresses specific impediments like poverty or child marriages via cause-effect.
annotations.comment: Outline sub-point lists measure descriptively without analytical depth; include reasoning on impact to strengthen argument.

annotations.anchor_quote: Article 25, and Article 38 NV
annotations.correction: Article 25-A (right to education) and Article 38 (social justice) of the Constitution of Pakistan.
annotations.comment: References to articles lack full source attribution and unclear 'NV'; specify reliable source for verifiability.

annotations.anchor_quote: Secondly, when female gets education, she secures economic sustenance for herself.
annotations.correction: Secondly, when a female gets education, she secures economic sustenance for herself.
annotations.comment: Add indefinite article 'a' before 'female' to correct grammatical structure.

annotations.anchor_quote: provide for them According to UNFD
annotations.correction: provide for them. According to UNFD
annotations.comment: Insert period after 'them' to properly separate the sentence from the following citation.

annotations.anchor_quote: Thirdly, Females when edu- cated, are fully aware of their rights.
annotations.correction: Thirdly, females when educated are fully aware of their rights.
annotations.comment: Lowercase 'Females', remove unnecessary comma and hyphen for proper sentence structure.

annotations.anchor_quote: This protects her from blindly following someone just because they materially provide for them
annotations.comment: Remove repetition of 'blindly following' concept already stated earlier; consolidate into one point.

annotations.anchor_quote: Hence, when the female is educated, she comes out of this vicious cycle of dependence and makes decisions for herself just as she likes.
annotations.comment: Add critical analysis via cause-effect links; explain how breaking dependence prevents blind obedience specifically.

annotations.anchor_quote: women make 230% of world parliaments.
annotations.correction: women make up approximately 26% of world parliaments.
annotations.comment: Statistic exceeds 100% and is impossible; verify from reliable UN source and correct to accurate figure.

annotations.anchor_quote: plass amendment to protect women
annotations.correction: pass amendments to protect women
annotations.comment: Spelling error 'plass' should be 'pass'; correct for proper readability and professionalism.

annotations.anchor_quote: menace of povertythat hinders worven education.
annotations.correction: menace of poverty that hinders women education.
annotations.comment: Missing space after 'poverty' and typo 'worven'; add space and correct to 'women' for clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: participate fully in the political process. Unfortunately, according to United Nations report women make 230% of world parliaments. Yet, there are women, although handful, that participate in the political process
annotations.correction: participate fully in the political process. Unfortunately, according to United Nations report women make up approximately 26% of world parliaments. Yet, some women become
annotations.comment: Repetition of 'participate in the political process'; rephrase second instance to avoid redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: Despite the need and significance of female education, there are some mounting impediments that paralyze the process of women education.
annotations.correction: However, despite the need and significance of female education, mounting impediments such as...
annotations.comment: Abrupt transition from benefits to impediments; add smoother connector like 'However' for better flow.

annotations.anchor_quote: She would legal facilitations to protect them such as Protection of Women Against Workplace Harassment, (2010), Acid Control and Acid Crime Prevention Act (2011) and Women Property Protection Right (2020).
annotations.correction: She would utilize legal facilitations to protect them such as Protection of Women Against Workplace Harassment (2010), Acid Control and Acid Crime Prevention Act (2011), and Women Property Protection Rights (2020).
annotations.comment: Insert verb 'utilize' and fix punctuation around law names for complete sentence and clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: And uneducated female can not be expected of knowing these rights but on educated female in Pakistan would know
annotations.correction: An uneducated female cannot be expected to know these rights, but an educated female in Pakistan would know
annotations.comment: Add articles 'An' and 'to', change 'can not' to 'cannot', replace 'of knowing' with 'to know', and fix 'on' to comma for grammatical correctness.

annotations.anchor_quote: any unjustice against her personhood.
annotations.correction: any injustice against her personhood.
annotations.comment: Correct misspelling 'unjustice' to 'injustice'.

annotations.anchor_quote: She starts to think and live bey and them. She starts to believe that customs are only here to margin- alize cher
annotations.correction: She starts to think and live beyond them, believing that customs are only here to marginalize her
annotations.comment: Correct 'bey and' to 'beyond', 'margin- alize cher' to 'marginalize her', and combine repetitive phrases for smoother flow.

annotations.anchor_quote: Fourthly, educated females become immune to evil social customs and the absurd stereotypes.
annotations.comment: Strengthen descriptive claim by adding cause-effect analysis on how education specifically builds immunity to customs.

annotations.anchor_quote: In Pakistan, there 14 million girls out of 26 million total OOSC.
annotations.correction: In Pakistan, there are 14 million girls out of 26 million total OOSC.
annotations.comment: Missing verb 'are' after 'there'; add 'are' for subject-verb agreement.

annotations.anchor_quote: This is a great social customs that destroys, a female, socially, sexually and health wise
annotations.correction: This is a great social custom that destroys a female socially, sexually, and health-wise.
annotations.comment: Incorrect singular 'customs' and awkward comma phrasing; change to singular 'custom' and remove unnecessary commas while using hyphen for 'health-wise'.

annotations.anchor_quote: In Pakistan 18% of the girls of the age 13-17 are married very young and become "Child brides".
annotations.comment: Statistic on child marriages lacks cited source; attribute to a reliable source like UNICEF for verifiability.

annotations.anchor_quote: Here, it is a big obst acl which needs to be eradicated at all costs for the safety of women and their progress.
annotations.comment: Point on child marriages is descriptive without cause-effect analysis; add explanation of how it perpetuates blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: Owing to which there are 132 million Out of School girls worldwide.
annotations.comment: Global statistic on out-of-school girls lacks source attribution; cite UNESCO or similar reliable source.

annotations.anchor_quote: It is the women who nurture and polishes the next generation.
annotations.correction: It is the women who nurture and polish the next generation.
annotations.comment: Correct subject-verb agreement by changing 'polishes' to 'polish' since 'women' is plural.

annotations.anchor_quote: how will sche will ensure good brought up of her Children.
annotations.correction: how she will ensure the proper upbringing of her children.
annotations.comment: Fix typo 'sche' to 'she', remove duplicate 'will', use 'upbringing' instead of 'brought up', and lowercase 'Children'.

annotations.anchor_quote: requires resources to sponsers it.
annotations.correction: requires resources to sponsor it.
annotations.comment: Correct spelling error by changing 'sponsers' to 'sponsor'.

annotations.anchor_quote: According to World Bank report, 38% of women across the globe earn less than $ 1.9/day
annotations.correction: According to [specific World Bank report, year], 38% of women...
annotations.comment: Specify full source details like report title and year to make the statistic verifiable.

annotations.anchor_quote: such low means and eventually
annotations.correction: such low means and eventually drop out of education.
annotations.comment: Complete the abrupt ending with a clear conclusion to the idea for better flow.

annotations.anchor_quote: Globally the digital divide is 60% out of which girls/females are 51% In Pakistan, situation is even worse. The to digital divide is 58% whereas for girls it is 24%
annotations.correction: Cite sources like ITU or UN Women.
annotations.comment: Source for factual data on digital divide not mentioned; attribute to reliable sources.

annotations.anchor_quote: support girls education
annotations.correction: girls' education
annotations.comment: Missing possessive apostrophe; use 'girls' education' for correct grammar.

annotations.anchor_quote: The to digital divide is 58%
annotations.correction: The digital divide is 58%
annotations.comment: Typographical error; correct 'to' to nothing for proper phrasing.

annotations.anchor_quote: access turainternet HA
annotations.correction: access to internet
annotations.comment: Typographical and spelling errors; correct to 'access to internet'.

annotations.anchor_quote: persue their
annotations.correction: pursue their
annotations.comment: Spelling error; correct to 'pursue their'.

annotations.anchor_quote: in order to persue their
annotations.correction: Add concluding sentence linking to education impacts.
annotations.comment: Paragraph ends abruptly; add summary or transition to next idea.

annotations.anchor_quote: This shows that more than half of the girls population in Pakistan does not even have access turainternet HA which shuts the door of online learning world on them completely.
annotations.correction: Add cause-effect analysis on obedience.
annotations.comment: Descriptive stats lack critical analysis; explain how digital divide perpetuates blind obedience.

annotations.anchor_quote: provision of education in for flung areas.
annotations.correction: provision of education in far-flung areas.
annotations.comment: Correct 'for flung' to 'far-flung' to fix spelling error and improve readability.

annotations.anchor_quote: for 2030 preses on the issue of female education.
annotations.correction: for 2030 presses on the issue of female education.
annotations.comment: Correct 'preses' to 'presses' to fix spelling error.

annotations.anchor_quote: that protect woman
annotations.correction: that protect women
annotations.comment: Change 'woman' to 'women' for subject-verb agreement and plural consistency.

annotations.anchor_quote: In SDC: 4 there is goal to ensure free education
annotations.correction: In SDG 4 there is a goal to ensure inclusive and equitable quality education
annotations.comment: Correct 'SDC: 4' to 'SDG 4' and attribute precisely; SDG 4 focuses on quality education for all.

annotations.anchor_quote: Government must launch such initiatives as well as build the public-private partnership in order to streamline the process
annotations.comment: Back recommendation with cause-effect analysis or evidence on how PPP addresses digital divide effectively.

annotations.anchor_quote: eliminate therose hurdles in order to facilitate the famele education,
annotations.correction: eliminate those hurdles in order to facilitate female education,
annotations.comment: Correct spelling errors 'therose' to 'those' and 'famele' to 'female' for accuracy.

annotations.anchor_quote: Firstly, there is need to understand that women educational empowerment
annotations.correction: First, there is a need to understand that women's educational empowerment
annotations.comment: Add article 'a' after 'is need', possessive 'women's', and change 'Firstly' to 'First' for proper grammar.

annotations.anchor_quote: There is a need to understand that women educational empowerment is actually "human development". There is a need to understand that women,
annotations.correction: There is a need to understand that women's educational empowerment is human development; otherwise, women will only burden the world.
annotations.comment: Repeated phrase 'There is a need to understand'; combine into one sentence to eliminate redundancy.

annotations.anchor_quote: Safety of homes. However, there are certain ways, policy measures and pragmatic decisions
annotations.correction: homes. To address these security concerns, however, there are certain ways, policy measures, and pragmatic decisions
annotations.comment: Abrupt shift from security issues to solutions; insert linking phrase for smoother transition.

annotations.anchor_quote: Women make up 57% of NEET (Not in Employment, Education or training).
annotations.correction: Women make up 57% of NEET (Not in Employment, Education or training) [e.g., ILO/World Bank report].
annotations.comment: Source for 57% NEET statistic is not mentioned; cite reliable source to verify claim.

annotations.anchor_quote: Secondly, there is need to make efforts for women education and skill development.
annotations.correction: Secondly, there is a need to make efforts for women's education and skill development.
annotations.comment: Add 'a' before 'need' and possessive 's' to 'women'; correct article and preposition usage.

annotations.anchor_quote: This would not only arest many physical, seval, and health issues from the lives of women
annotations.correction: This would not only arrest many physical, sexual, and health issues from the lives of women
annotations.comment: Correct spelling 'arest' to 'arrest' and 'seval' to 'sexual' for accurate language.

annotations.anchor_quote: This protects the women as well as ensure her respect and equality in marital bond as well which is need of the hour.
annotations.correction: This protects women and ensures their respect and equality in the marital bond, which is the need of the hour.
annotations.comment: Fix subject-verb agreement 'ensure' to 'ensures', pronoun 'her' to 'their', add 'the' before 'marital bond' and 'need'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Hence it is not just enough to educate them but also equip them with the right skills (so that they can earn from it and secure their economic independence.
annotations.correction: Hence educating alone is insufficient; equip with skills to earn income, breaking economic dependence and fostering independent decision-making that ends blind obedience.
annotations.comment: Argument is descriptive; add cause-effect analysis linking skills to reduced obedience and societal benefits.

annotations.anchor_quote: To conclude, it can be deduced that embellishing the female mind with education will protect her from blind compliance. She will rationalize before she does anything which will not only make her better, but world better. There are grave impediments that stops women education process but there is nothing that can not be changed and with attidumal change and policy implementation educating women can be come much more convenient not just for their own person hood but for the progress of the world.
annotations.correction: Revise to include a forward-looking statement.
annotations.comment: Conclusion restates thesis and summarizes but ends abruptly without thoughtful forward-looking vision; add a call to action for implementation.

annotations.anchor_quote: Article 25(A) dolares women deserve equal education rights.
annotations.correction: Article 25(A) declares women deserve equal education rights.
annotations.comment: Spelling error 'dolares' should be 'declares'; correct for clarity and accuracy.

annotations.anchor_quote: There are grave impediments that stops women education process
annotations.correction: There are grave impediments that stop women's education process
annotations.comment: Subject-verb agreement error 'impediments that stops'; change to 'impediments that stop' and add possessive 'women's'.

annotations.anchor_quote: with attidumal change and policy implementation educating women can be come much more convenient not just for their own person hood
annotations.correction: with attitudinal change and policy implementation, educating women can become much more convenient, not just for their own personhood
annotations.comment: Misspellings 'attidumal' to 'attitudinal', 'be come' to 'become', 'person hood' to 'personhood'; add commas for better flow.

annotations.anchor_quote: Hence, by applying, rather enforcing these laws, much of the edu- cation provision problems for women will alleviate. To conclude,
annotations.correction: Hence, by applying, rather than enforcing these laws, many of the education provision problems for women will be alleviated. In conclusion,
annotations.comment: Abrupt transition to conclusion and paragraph ends suddenly; add 'than' and smooth linker for better flow.
